title: Otophora pinnata
taxonomy: Otophora pinnata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. Otophora pinnata is further classified into the genus Otophora and the order Gentianales. The plant is characterized by its unique leaves and flowers, which contribute to its classification. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Otophora pinnata is closely related to other flowering plants within this group.
uses: Otophora pinnata has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Its roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and cough. The plant is also known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. In some cultures, Otophora pinnata is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health.

constituents: Otophora pinnata, also known as the African bushwillow, contains various active constituents, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The flavonoids present in Otophora pinnata, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are known for their ability to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress.
